sourceStatus.publicationPolicy: No long SEC source text is embedded; evidence uses short digests and source URL anchors.

summaryKo.oneLine: WASTE MANAGEMENT INC의 공시 핵심을 한국어로 요약했습니다.

keyPoints.text: 1분기 영업현금흐름이 전년 동기 대비 24% 증가해 15억 달러를 기록했습니다.
조정 영업 EBITDA는 5.9% 성장하며 마진이 70포인트 확대됐습니다.
주주 환원으로 배당금 및 자사주 매입을 통해 7억 3,000만 달러를 분배했습니다.

riskChanges.text: 기상 악화 영향이 분기 실적에 부정적으로 작용했으나 WM 팀과 사업 모델의 회복력으로 상쇄됐습니다.

businessChanges.text: 재활용·재생에너지 사업의 합산 영업 EBITDA가 5,100만 달러 증가했으며, 조정 기준으로는 4,900만 달러 성장했습니다.
신규 재활용 시설 3곳을 완공해 성장 시장에서 핵심 처리 용량을 추가 확보했습니다.
현장 인력 유지율 개선 투자와 기술·자동화 활용을 통해 비용 절감을 지속 추진하고 있습니다.

financialHighlights.text: 조정 영업 EBITDA는 5.9% 성장, 마진은 70포인트 개선됐습니다.
영업현금흐름이 15억 달러로 전년 동기 대비 24% 증가했습니다.
재활용·재생에너지 부문 영업 EBITDA가 조정 기준 4,900만 달러 성장했으며, 재생 천연가스 생산 증가가 기여했습니다.
주주 환원 규모는 7억 3,000만 달러로 1분기 잉여현금흐름의 대부분이 배분됐습니다.

watchItems.text: 연간 재무 전망을 재확인했으며, 경기 환경 변화에 대응할 유연한 재무 구조를 유지하고 있습니다.
재활용·재생에너지 성장 프로젝트 기여가 확대되는 추세로, 지속가능성 부문의 이익 기여 증가가 핵심 모멘텀입니다.

evidence.sourceTextDigest: EX-99.1 2 tm2612889d1_ex99-1.htm EXHIBIT 99.1 Exhibit 99.1 WM Announces First Quarter 2026 Earnings Strong Performance Drives Cash Flow from Operations 24% Higher to $1.5 Billion The Company Reaffirms its Full-Year Financial Outlook WM Completes Three Recycling Facilities Adding Key Capacity in Growing Markets Houston — April 28 , 2026 — WM (NYSE: WM) today announced financial results for the quarter ended March 31, 2026. Three Months Ended Three Months Ended March 31, 2026 (in millions, except per share amounts) March 31, 2025 (in millions, except per share amounts) As Reported As  Normalized numeric aliases: $1.5 Billion = 15억 달러; $1.5 = 1.5달러; $6,227 = 6,227달러; $6,018 = 6,018달러; $1,113 = 1,113달러; $1,118 = 1,118달러; $1,013 = 1,013달러; $1,059 = 1,059달러; $1,853 = 1,853달러; $637 = 637달러; $1.67 = 1.67달러; 1,013 million = 10억 1,300만 달러; 1,750 million = 17억 5,000만 달러; 1,059 million = 10억 5,900만 달러; 1,848 million = 18억 4,800만 달러; 1,853 million = 18억 5,300만 달러; 1,704 million = 17억 400만 달러.

and cash flow results in the quarter achieved our expectations, reflecting the strength of the WM team and the resilience of our business model,” said Jim Fish, WM’s CEO. “Disciplined pricing, cost optimization and contributions from sustainability growth projects led to first quarter adjusted operating EBITDA growth of 5.9% and margin expansion of 70 basis points despite a challenging quarter of weather impacts. (a) The momentum in our business, combined with our confidence in our ability to execute on our plan for the balance of the year, sets us up to achieve the full-year financial outlook we provided last quarter.” Fish continued, “Our start to 2026 strengthens our conviction in the ability to harvest the benefits of our strategic investments in recycling, renewable energy, a medical waste platform, technology, and our fleet, as free cash flow nearly doubled in the quarter  Normalized numeric aliases: 70 basis points = 70포인트.

to prior year. (a) We allocated the majority of first quarter free cash flow to shareholders, returning nearly $730 million through dividends and share repurchases during the quarter. Our balance sheet remains flexible, reflecting our financial discipline and positioning WM to capitalize on opportunities in any economic environment.” KEY HIGHLIGHTS FOR the fIRST quarter OF 2026 · Adjusted operating EBITDA grew 5.9%, with margin expanding 70 basis points. (a) Performance was led by the Collection and Disposal business, driven by disciplined price execution, operating cost control, and continued optimization of business mix. · Collection and Disposal operating EBITDA grew by $154 million and margin expanded 190 basis points. On an adjusted basis,  Normalized numeric aliases: $730 million = 7억 3,000만 달러; $154 million = 1억 5,400만 달러; $118 million = 1억 1,800만 달러; $730 = 730달러; $154 = 154달러; $118 = 118달러; 70 basis points = 70포인트; 190 basis points = 190포인트; 110 basis points = 110포인트.

the Company invests in making improvements in frontline retention and leverages technology and automation to reduce costs. (a) · Together, operating EBITDA in the Recycling and Renewable Energy businesses grew $51 million, or $49 million on an adjusted basis, driven by increased renewable natural gas production from growth projects as well as higher recycling volumes and benefits from automation projects. (a)(e) · Operating EBITDA grew by 18.4%, or 11.6% on an adjusted basis, in the Healthcare Solutions business, driven by effective SG&A cost management and synergy capture. (a) · Revenue grew 3.5%, driven by core price of 6.3% and collection and disposal yield of 3.9%. In addition to strong execution on pricing, revenue growth was driven by increased volumes in the Recycling and Renewable Energy businesses from completed growth projects. (f) · Collection and disposal volume declined  Normalized numeric aliases: $51 million = 5,100만 달러; $49 million = 4,900만 달러; $51 = 51달러; $49 = 49달러.
